events.image_prompt: Cramped brutalist apartment, harsh grey dawn light cutting through single window, concrete walls closing in, minimalist prison-like room, oppressive geometry
events.text: The alarm dies three seconds before your eyes open. This precision disturbs you, though you cannot say why. The ceiling crack resembles a question mark. Or it always has. Memory offers no testimony. The letter on your table assigns you to something you never requested. Perhaps you did request it. Perhaps requesting and not requesting have become the same act.

events.image_prompt: Official document with red wax seal broken, bureaucratic text, Department of Cohesion emblem casting shadow, grey paper under cold fluorescent light, sense of violation
events.text: Division of Trajectory Analysis. Floor 11. Station 47-C. The words carry the authority of axioms. Monitor variance. Maintain coherence. You search your memory for the act of application. The search itself feels borrowed. As if someone else is searching through you.

events.image_prompt: Underground metro station, workers in grey moving in perfect unison like clockwork, fluorescent lights creating harsh shadows, brutalist architecture crushing human scale
events.text: The crowd moves with mathematical precision. Bodies as variables in an equation already solved. You have stood here before. Not yesterday. Not last week. Before time began counting itself. The station remembers you better than you remember the station.

events.image_prompt: Monolithic brutalist government tower blotting out grey sky, concrete facade without windows, entrance like a mouth, oppressive scale
events.text: The Department does not rise from the earth. It has always been here, and the earth arranged itself around it. The security apparatus measures something inside your skull. The pause extends. In that half-second, you understand that hesitation is already guilt. The green light absolves nothing.

events.image_prompt: Mass of identical grey-clothed workers, one woman's face frozen mid-blink, eyes not quite synchronized with the others, uncanny valley effect
events.text: The woman's eyelid descends a fraction too slowly. Or too quickly. The desynchronization lasts less than a heartbeat. When her eyes align with the others, you realize the disruption was not in her rhythm. It was in your perception of her rhythm. The error is yours. The error has always been yours.

events.image_prompt: Claustrophobic office cubicle, terminal displaying cascading probability data, no windows, fluorescent light humming, prison-like workspace
events.text: The dashboard presents human lives as probability distributions. SIMULATION SUBJECTS, the header declares. They are not real, you are informed. The question of whether you are real enough to make that determination goes unasked. Asking would be variance. Variance must be corrected.

events.image_prompt: Child's profile data on screen, branching decision trees like neural pathways, innocent face surrounded by probability calculations
events.text: Subject 7-1201. Female. Nine years old. She has told a lie to her mother. Seventeen possible futures branch from this moment. In one branch, guilt teaches her conscience. In another, successful deception teaches her strategy. The system recommends collapse. Childhood, it seems, is inefficient.

events.image_prompt: Heavily redacted government document on screen, black censorship bars, warnings in red text, bureaucratic language hiding truth
events.text: WARNING: Synchronization effects documented in 0.3% of analysts. Symptoms include: déjà vu (chronic), identity drift (progressive), recursive awareness (terminal). The parenthetical modifiers suggest these are not bugs. They are features operating outside acceptable parameters.

events.image_prompt: Single illuminated button on dark interface, particle injection system controls, clinical and cold, instrument of erasure
events.text: Your finger descends. The button accepts the pressure with mechanical indifference. Subject 7-1201's probability cloud collapses to a single line. She will forget the lie. She will forget that forgetting was forced upon her. You feel relief. Then your stomach turns. Relief and nausea are not opposites. They are the same recognition arriving through different doors.

events.image_prompt: Warning indicators escalating on screen, amber alerts pulsing, deviation percentage climbing in real-time
events.text: The deviation metric climbs: 8.4%. Your supervisor's message arrives with the inevitability of a mathematical proof. 'Variance requires correction.' Your hesitation is measured in milliseconds. The system logs milliseconds. Mercy, it seems, can be quantified. And therefore eliminated.

events.image_prompt: Young man's anguished face on monitor, probability clouds fracturing into chaos, data corruption spreading like infection
events.text: Subject 7-6634. Stanislav Novák. Twenty-three years old. He has arrived at the question that ends all questions: 'Are my thoughts mine?' Deviation spikes to 32%. Then 41%. The screen flickers. In the corruption, you see your own designation. ANALYST 47-C. You are also a number being monitored. The thought is not new. You have thought it before. Many times before.

events.image_prompt: Split screen showing subject's neural chaos and building-wide synchronization spike, systems bleeding into each other
events.text: Blood. Your nose is bleeding. You have not moved. Twenty-three floors above, someone collapses. The event log shows simultaneous timestamp. SYNCHRONIZATION SPIKE DETECTED. The child's suffering has opened a seam in reality. Or reality was always torn, and you are only now bleeding through the gap.

events.image_prompt: Stabilized neural patterns on screen, green confirmation indicators, false peace restored
events.text: Stanislav stops asking. The building's electromagnetic hum returns to baseline. Your headache evaporates. COHERENCE RESTORED, the system confirms. You have murdered a question. The corpse will decompose quietly in Stanislav's amygdala. He will sleep better. You will not.

events.image_prompt: Subject's lips forming words on screen - 'Am I real?' - camera perspective suddenly inverted
events.text: Stanislav's lips move. 'What if someone is watching me?' The screen glitches. The camera angle inverts. For one frame, you see your own workstation from behind. From above. From the perspective of something that should not exist. The angle is impossible. The angle is true.

events.image_prompt: Office cubicle from impossible overhead angle, as if viewed through surveillance that should not exist
events.text: Your chair. Your desk. Your monitor. All in their correct positions. The office is empty. It has always been empty. But you are not alone. Your own terminal betrays you with a flash: TRAJECTORY ANALYST 47-C - STABILITY 74% - VARIANCE ACCEPTABLE. The screen goes dark. The message was not for you. It was about you. For someone else.

events.image_prompt: Reflection in dark metro window, grey city lights smearing past, face watching itself watch the city
events.text:
Your reflection observes the city. Or the city observes you through your reflection. The distinction has collapsed. The automated voice: 'Welcome to your first day.'

Silence.

'Welcome to your first day.'

The repetition is not an error. The error is believing there was ever a second day. Or that days are countable things. Or that you are the one counting.
